input.headline: Inside the Amazon’s sacred food rituals few outsiders see

raw.headline: Inside the Amazon’s sacred food rituals few outsiders see

winner: The Amazonian tribe held a sacred food ritual where they offered a feast to the forest spirits, but the ritual was ruined when the spirits refused to eat because they were strictly gluten-free.

candidates.text: I tried to attend an Amazonian sacred food ritual, but I was told I couldn't bring my gluten-free crackers — apparently, the jungle doesn't recognize dietary restrictions.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on sacred rituals with a modern, relatable joke about dietary restrictions."}

candidates.text: Why did the Amazonian shaman refuse to share his secret recipe? Because he didn’t want anyone to "leaf" his sacred ingredients out on the table!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'leaf' and 'reveal,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity about Amazonian rituals. It also misses some punchiness."}

candidates.text: I joined an Amazon sacred food ritual and all I got was this luscious, leaf-wrapped feast—turns out they don’t serve any of it in the buff.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has some wordplay with 'in the buff' but lacks clear connection to Amazonian rituals and feels a bit forced."}

candidates.text: Inside the Amazon's sacred food rituals few outsiders see — and even fewer can pronounce the names of the dishes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific, relatable punchline about pronunciation."}

candidates.text: The Amazonian tribes are famous for their sacred, mystical food rituals, but it’s mostly just a very intense way of trying to figure out which one of them actually stole the communal mango.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a humorous twist on sacred rituals. However, it slightly undercuts the mystical tone of the headline with a more mundane explanation."}

candidates.text: The Amazonian tribe held a sacred food ritual where they offered a feast to the forest spirits, but the ritual was ruined when the spirits refused to eat because they were strictly gluten-free.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a modern twist (gluten-free), but the humor is somewhat mild and relies on a niche cultural context."}

candidates.text: The Amazon tribe's sacred food ritual involves chewing their own spit into a paste for energy—until they realized they could just eat regular food and save on therapy sessions.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor falls flat due to an awkward comparison (therapy sessions) that doesn't naturally follow from the premise."}

candidates.text: The Amazonian tribes are so protective of their sacred food rituals that they don't let anyone see them, but honestly, it’s just a matter of keeping their secrets under wraps.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 3.0, "reason": "Lacks surprise and specificity; the joke is generic and relies on a cliché punchline that doesn't engage with the headline's premise."}
